name: baptistry
summary: A chamber built around a font or sunken pool for rites of initiation — baptism, cleansing, rebirth. Wet stone, echo, candles reflected in still water.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: square or octagonal — the pool is centered and approached from all sides

accent.purpose: the pool surround — a contrasting border

primary_palette.note: a sunken pool with candelabra at each corner
a standing font flanked by pillars — for infant baptism or head-anointing
a pool watched over by an effigy of the patron on a central plinth
a small font with kneelers — personal rite, not ceremonial spectacle

story_prompts: a rite in progress — a robed initiate stepping into the water, priest with raised hand
empty and still — the pool glass-flat, candles doubled in reflection
the water wrong — dark, steaming, or algal; something has spoiled the rite
aftermath — wet footprints leading to a side door, candles guttered
a secret chamber beneath the pool — drained, the tiled basin lifted aside

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: reflective and clear (0.4-0.5) — warm candlelight caught and doubled on the water

anti_patterns: the pool or font is centered — the room is built around it
approachable from multiple sides — no single forced axis
never fill with clutter; the water should dominate the sightline
